content.problemStatement:
It is well known fact in mathematics that the sum of the 333 angles in a triangle is 180180180 degrees.

You had a triangle, but unfortunately you only remember the 1st1^{st}1st and 2nd2^{nd}2nd angles of it, and you have forgotten the 3rd3^{rd}3rd one.

Given that the first angle was AAA, and the second was BBB, can you figure out the third one? All angles are integers measured in degrees.


Input Format

The first and only line of input contains 222 integers - AAA and BBB.


Output Format
Print a single integer - the measure of the 3rd3^{rd}3rd angle (in degrees).

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
It is an equilateral triangle, and has all angles equal.
